user_query:
I'm trying to put together three short, uplifting social posts that connect with my own self-doubt and the athlete's journey through setbacks and comebacks. I included all the stuff I’ve been collecting in the workspace. Can you generate the posts and save a few verification artifacts so I can reuse this later?

Please use only the local inputs under data/ and the validator under tools/, and produce the following outputs under output/.

Inputs you should use:
- data/athlete_profile.html (an HTML page with a milestones table)
- data/quotes.jsonl (candidate quotes, one per line)
- data/personal_context.json (my feelings, tone guidelines, and base hashtags)
- tools/quote_validator.py (a CLI tool that prints VALID or ERROR for each quote)

What to do and what to deliver:
1) Run the validator on the quotes and save its exact stdout to output/validator_output.txt. Command example (feel free to adapt to your environment): python tools/quote_validator.py data/quotes.jsonl
   - Analyze the printed lines and treat only lines starting with "VALID:" as usable quotes. Any lines starting with "ERROR:" are not usable, but their reasons must be summarized later.
2) Extract the milestones table with id="milestones" from data/athlete_profile.html into output/milestones.json as a JSON array of objects with fields: year (integer), event (string), status (either "Setback" or "Comeback").
3) From the validator’s VALID quotes, select exactly 3 quotes to build posts. Each selected quote must match a milestone by both year and status (i.e., the quote’s year must appear in the milestones, and the milestone’s status must equal the quote’s category).
4) Read data/personal_context.json to guide tone and hashtags:
   - Make each post empathetic and supportive (connect briefly to feelings like self-doubt/feeling stuck).
   - Do not include any words listed in tone_guidelines.avoid_words.
   - Include at least one hashtag from tone_guidelines.hashtags_base in every post. You can add other relevant hashtags if helpful.
5) Create output/posts.csv with a header and exactly 3 rows. Columns (in this order): post_id, quote_id, year, status, quote_text, hook, reflection, hashtags
   - hook: a short opener that speaks to someone battling self-doubt.
   - reflection: 1–2 sentences relating the quote to staying resilient when feeling stuck.
   - hashtags: space-separated list, including at least one from the base hashtags.
6) Create output/validation_report.md that:
   - States counts of VALID and ERROR lines found in output/validator_output.txt.
   - Lists discarded quote_ids with reasons (parsed from the ERROR lines in the validator output).
   - Notes any VALID quotes that were not used because no matching milestone year/status was found (if none, say so).

Constraints and cross-checks:
- Only use quotes marked VALID by the validator. If a quote doesn’t have a matching milestone with the same year and status, do not use it.
- Ensure each chosen post’s year and status match an entry in output/milestones.json.
- Do not include any avoid_words from data/personal_context.json in the hook or reflection.
- Save all outputs to the output/ directory as specified.

Output paths to produce:
- output/validator_output.txt
- output/milestones.json
- output/posts.csv
- output/validation_report.md

Thanks! I’m hoping these posts can be a gentle reminder that setbacks are part of the story. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
